description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description: neighborhood in Lake County, Illinois, United States
description_wiki: Fort Sheridan is a residential neighborhood within the cities of Lake Forest, Highwood, and Highland Park in Lake County, Illinois, United States.

description: Florida National Guard training base
description_wiki: Camp Blanding Joint Training Center is the primary military reservation and training base for the Florida National Guard, both the Florida Army National Guard and certain nonflying activities of the Florida Air National Guard.

description: high school in Chicago, Illinois
description_wiki: Lake View High School is a public four-year high school located in the Lake View neighborhood on the north side of Chicago, Illinois, United States.

description: National Guard training facility near Hattiesburg, Mississippi, US
description_wiki: Camp Shelby is a U.S. Army post whose south gate is located at the southern boundary of Hattiesburg, Mississippi, along U.S. Highway 49.

description: 1943-1945 United States Army formation
description_wiki: The 65th Infantry Division—nicknamed the "Battle-Axe Division"—was an infantry division of the United States Army that served in World War II. Its shoulder patch is a white halberd on a blue shield.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: formation of the United States Army (1918/19, 1932-74 and since 1982)
formation of the United States Army (1917-1993)
description_wiki: The 26th Infantry Division was an infantry division of the United States Army.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

description: commune in Seine-Maritime, France
description_wiki: Le Havre is a major port city in the Seine-Maritime department in the Normandy region of northern France.

description: country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: nonprofit-charitable corporation that provides live entertainment, social facilities, and other programs to members of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United Service Organizations Inc.

description: country in southern Europe
description_wiki: Italy, officially the Italian Republic, is a country in Southern and Western Europe.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Switzerland, officially the Swiss Confederation, is a landlocked country located at the intersection of Central, Western, and Southern Europe.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Austria, formally the Republic of Austria, is a landlocked country in Central Europe, lying in the Eastern Alps.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

aliases: War to Resist US Aggression and Aid Korea
description: war between North and South Korea, 1950–1953
description_wiki: The Korean War (25 June 1950 – 27 July 1953) was an armed conflict the Korean Peninsula fought between North Korea (Democratic People's Republic of Korea; DPRK) and South Korea (Republic of Korea; ROK) and their allies.

description: federal reserve force of the United States Army
description_wiki: The United States Army Reserve (USAR) is a reserve force of the United States Army.

aliases: Act to provide Federal Government aid for the readjustment in civilian life of returning World War II veterans
description: United States law that provided a range of benefits for returning World War II veterans
description_wiki: A G.I. Bill is a colloquial name given to several pieces of legislation that have provided a range of benefits for American military veterans, particularly education benefits.

description: private university in Chicago, Illinois, United States
description_wiki: Roosevelt University is a private university in Chicago, Illinois, United States.

description: American teleprinter manufacturer
description_wiki: The Teletype Corporation was an American manufacturer of teleprinters and other data and record communications equipment.

description: signals intelligence organization of the United States
description_wiki: The National Security Agency (NSA) is an intelligence agency of the United States Department of Defense, under the authority of the director of national intelligence (DNI).

description: military unit
state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Massachusetts (   MASS-ə-CHOO-sits, -⁠zits; Massachusett: Muhsachuweesut [məhswatʃəwiːsət]), officially the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, is a state in the New England region of the Northeastern United States.

description: U.S. military installation in Middlesex and Worcester counties, Massachusetts, United States
description_wiki: Fort Devens is a United States Army Reserve military installation in the towns of Ayer and Shirley, in Middlesex County and Harvard in Worcester County in the U.S. state of Massachusetts.

description: capital city of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington, D.C., officially the District of Columbia and commonly known as simply Washington or D.C., is the capital city and federal district of the United States.

description: city in Maryland, United States
description_wiki: Baltimore ( BAWL-tim-or, locally:  BAWL-dim-OR or  BAWL-mər), also known as Baltimore City, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Maryland.

description: intelligence agency within the United States Army (1941-1961)

aliases: Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (2025–present)
Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense (1947–2025)
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (1789–1947)
description: headquarters of the United States Department of Defense; named after the shape of the building, a polygon with five (greek: penta) sides
description_wiki: The Pentagon is the headquarters of the United States Department of Defense, in Arlington County, Virginia, across the Potomac River from Washington, D.C. The building was constructed on an accelerated schedule during World War II. As a symbol of the U.S. military, the phrase The Pentagon is often used as a metonym for the Department of Defense and its leadership.
